recovery.articlesIntro: Curated science-backed research and practical takeaways for optimizing your training, nutrition, and recovery.

quotes: Muscle protein synthesis peaks 24–72h after training. Recovery IS growth. — Schoenfeld, 2010
The body achieves what the mind believes. Consistency beats intensity.
10 sets/muscle/week is the minimum effective dose for hypertrophy. — Krieger, 2010
Sleep 7–9h: testosterone drops 15% with <5h sleep. — Leproult & Van Cauter, 2011
Progressive overload: add 2.5% load per week. Small jumps, massive results.
Creatine monohydrate is the most researched and effective supplement. 3–5g/day. — Buford et al., 2007
Your muscles don't know how much weight is on the bar — they only know tension.
2g protein/kg bodyweight optimizes recomp. Distribute across 4+ meals. — Morton et al., 2018
Discipline is choosing between what you want NOW and what you want MOST.
Training to failure isn't necessary for growth — 1-3 RIR is optimal. — Grgic et al., 2022
You don't need motivation every day; you need structure and repetition.
Technique first, load second: injury-free consistency wins.
Eat to perform today and recover for tomorrow.
One bad workout doesn't ruin progress; quitting the plan does.
Keep your foundations simple: sleep, protein, steps, water, and training.
